paragraph_text: Lately, I’ve been feeling really sad, stressed, and miserable, carrying a severe heaviness from my past hardships and trauma. Sleeping is incredibly difficult; I lie awake dwelling on stressors or wake up shaking from intense nighttime panic attacks and vivid nightmares. This constant insomnia leaves me completely exhausted, moody, and struggling with severe fatigue, poor concentration, and a disrupted appetite. My self-worth is low—I often forget my good qualities, regret past choices like my last marriage, and feel a deep sense of failure. Socially, my family can be judgmental, and though I have a loving boyfriend, I struggle with an internal urge to withdraw. I have to forcefully push myself to attend auditions or make it to appointments because a part of me just wants to hide away. While I try to survive and stay busy to avoid feeling that life isn't worth it, everything feels like an agonizing struggle.
explanation_text: The prediction for this message is severe. Words like “survive”, “marriage”, and “forget” signal ongoing hardship, regret over past relationships, and a sense of losing control—all cues that are linked to deeper depression. Mentioning the need to keep going when you want to hide shows persistent internal conflict, and reflecting on a lost marriage shows a heavy emotional load; admitting that memories slip highlights a feeling of helplessness. These points together push the assessment higher than mild or moderate. If the message had said “stay hopeful” instead of “survive”, the model might have leaned toward a milder level, because the language would suggest more acceptance and resilience. The way these phrases frame your experience directly influences how the tool evaluates the severity.

explanation_text: I assessed your sharing and it indicates a severe level of distress. The mention of “marriage” signals a painful past relationship that continues to weigh on your self‑view. The word “survive” shows that you feel compelled to keep going even when it feels too heavy, hinting at a sense of helplessness. “Auditions” reflects a pressure to perform or prove yourself in front of others, adding to the stress you describe. Together, these phrases point to strong emotional strain, ongoing pain, and a feeling of being trapped, which the model interpreted as a high risk of depression.
